trans-unit.source: __Click here to return to the exercise overview for module 3.__ ::: ::::
trans-unit.target: __Click here to return to the exercise overview for module 3.__ ::: ::::

trans-unit.source: Exercise 3: Data Queries
trans-unit.target: Exercise 3: Data Queries

trans-unit.source: Characteristics of the exercise
trans-unit.target: Characteristics of the exercise

trans-unit.source: :::{topic} __Aim of the exercise:__ ^^^ The aim of this exercise is to learn how to manipulate secondary data to generate insights. In this exercise, we will determine which buildings have been affected by a flood and apply a filter to determine which buildings are part of the critical infrastructure. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{topic} __Aim of the exercise:__ ^^^ The aim of this exercise is to learn how to manipulate secondary data to generate insights. In this exercise, we will determine which buildings have been affected by a flood and apply a filter to determine which buildings are part of the critical infrastructure. :::

trans-unit.source: Links to Wiki articles
trans-unit.target: Links to Wiki articles

trans-unit.source: [Geoprocessing - Clip](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_geoprocessing_wiki.html#clip)
trans-unit.target: [Geoprocessing - Clip](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_geoprocessing_wiki.html#clip)

trans-unit.source: Data
trans-unit.target: Data

trans-unit.source: Download all the datasets [here](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/Module_3/Exercise_3/Module_3_Exercise_3_Data_Queries.zip), save the folder on your computer, and unzip the file. The zip folder includes:
trans-unit.target: Download all the datasets [here](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/Module_3/Exercise_3/Module_3_Exercise_3_Data_Queries.zip), save the folder on your computer, and unzip the file. The zip folder includes:

trans-unit.source: `som_admbnda_adm2_ocha_20230308.shp`: This file contains information about the Somali administrative level 0-2, state, and operational zone level 1 and 2 boundary as shapefiles. The data can also be found on [HDX](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/cod-ab-som).
trans-unit.target: `som_admbnda_adm2_ocha_20230308.shp`: This file contains information about the Somali administrative level 0-2, state, and operational zone level 1 and 2 boundary as shapefiles. The data can also be found on [HDX](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/cod-ab-som).

trans-unit.source: `GF2_20231123_FloodExtent_BeledweyneCity_HiraanRegion.shp`: This shapefile illustrates satellite-detected surface waters in Beledweyne City, Beledweyne District, Hiraan Region, Somalia, on 12th of November 2023 at 07:32 UTC. The data is also available on [HDX](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/water-extent-in-beledweyne-city-beledweyne-district-hiraan-region-somalia-12-november-2023).
trans-unit.target: `GF2_20231123_FloodExtent_BeledweyneCity_HiraanRegion.shp`: This shapefile illustrates satellite-detected surface waters in Beledweyne City, Beledweyne District, Hiraan Region, Somalia, on 12th of November 2023 at 07:32 UTC. The data is also available on [HDX](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/water-extent-in-beledweyne-city-beledweyne-district-hiraan-region-somalia-12-november-2023).

trans-unit.source: `buildings_belet_weyne.geojson`: This dataset is downloaded using [HOT Export Tool](https://export.hotosm.org/v3/exports/new/describe) and contains information about buildings in the Beledweyne district.
trans-unit.target: `buildings_belet_weyne.geojson`: This dataset is downloaded using [HOT Export Tool](https://export.hotosm.org/v3/exports/new/describe) and contains information about buildings in the Beledweyne district.

trans-unit.source: The folder is called **Module_3_Exercise_3_Data_Queries** and contains the entire [standard folder structure](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projects_folder_structure_wiki.html#standard-folder-structure) with all data in the input folder.
trans-unit.target: The folder is called **Module_3_Exercise_3_Data_Queries** and contains the entire [standard folder structure](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projects_folder_structure_wiki.html#standard-folder-structure) with all data in the input folder.

trans-unit.source: Tasks
trans-unit.target: Tasks

trans-unit.source: Open QGIS and create a [new project](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projects_folder_structure_wiki.html#step-by-step-setting-up-a-new-qgis-project-from-scratch) by clicking on `Project` → `New`.
trans-unit.target: Open QGIS and create a [new project](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projects_folder_structure_wiki.html#step-by-step-setting-up-a-new-qgis-project-from-scratch) by clicking on `Project` → `New`.

trans-unit.source: Once the project is created, [save the project](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projects_folder_structure_wiki.html#save-project) in the **project folder** of the exercise **Module_3_Exercise_1_Queries_Somalia**. To do that click on `Project` → `Save as` and navigate to the folder. Name the project **Somalia_flood_affected_Beledweyne_2023**.
trans-unit.target: Once the project is created, [save the project](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projects_folder_structure_wiki.html#save-project) in the **project folder** of the exercise **Module_3_Exercise_1_Queries_Somalia**. To do that click on `Project` → `Save as` and navigate to the folder. Name the project **Somalia_flood_affected_Beledweyne_2023**.

trans-unit.source: To load the following files into your project, drag and drop them ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#open-vector-data-via-drag-and-drop)). Or click on `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Vector Layer`. Click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png) and navigate to the file. Select the file and click `Open`. Back in QGIS click `Add` ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#open-vector-data-via-layer-tab)).
trans-unit.target: To load the following files into your project, drag and drop them ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#open-vector-data-via-drag-and-drop)). Or click on `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Vector Layer`. Click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png) and navigate to the file. Select the file and click `Open`. Back in QGIS click `Add` ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#open-vector-data-via-layer-tab)).

trans-unit.source: `Buildings_Belete_Weyne.geojson`: A pop-up window will appear for this file and you will need to decide which data to import. Select the polygons.
trans-unit.target: `Buildings_Belete_Weyne.geojson`: A pop-up window will appear for this file and you will need to decide which data to import. Select the polygons.

trans-unit.source: Extract the district (adm2) from the administrative boundaries layer
trans-unit.target: Extract the district (adm2) from the administrative boundaries layer

trans-unit.source: First, we want to export the district __Beledweyne__ from the Hiraan region from `som_admbnda_adm2_ocha_20230308.shp` to have it as a stand-alone vector layer. To do that:
trans-unit.target: First, we want to export the district __Beledweyne__ from the Hiraan region from `som_admbnda_adm2_ocha_20230308.shp` to have it as a stand-alone vector layer. To do that:

trans-unit.source: Open the attribute table of `som_admbnda_adm2_ocha_20230308.shp` by right clicking on the layer → `Open Attribute Table`([Wiki Video](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_attribute_table_wiki.md)).
trans-unit.target: Open the attribute table of `som_admbnda_adm2_ocha_20230308.shp` by right clicking on the layer → `Open Attribute Table`([Wiki Video](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_attribute_table_wiki.md)).

trans-unit.source: Find the row of `Belet Weyne` and mark it by clicking on the number on the very left-hand side of the attribute table. The row will be highlighted in blue and the district will turn yellow on the map canvas. You can right-click on the row and click `Zoom to Feature`([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_attribute_table_wiki.html#zoom-in-on-a-specific-feature)).
trans-unit.target: Find the row of `Belet Weyne` and mark it by clicking on the number on the very left-hand side of the attribute table. The row will be highlighted in blue and the district will turn yellow on the map canvas. You can right-click on the row and click `Zoom to Feature`([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_attribute_table_wiki.html#zoom-in-on-a-specific-feature)).

trans-unit.source: Now right-click on the layer in the Layer Panel and click on `Export` → `Save Selected Features as`. We want to save Beledweyne as a GeoPackage, so adjust `Format` accordingly. Click on the three points and navigate to your **temp folder**. Here you can give the layer the name **AOI_Beledweyne** and click `Save`. Now click `Ok`([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_non_spatial_queries_wiki.html#save-selected-features-as-a-new-file)). In this exercise, we will not reproject the layers and work with the data in `ESPG:4326 - WGS84`.
trans-unit.target: Now right-click on the layer in the Layer Panel and click on `Export` → `Save Selected Features as`. We want to save Beledweyne as a GeoPackage, so adjust `Format` accordingly. Click on the three points and navigate to your **temp folder**. Here you can give the layer the name **AOI_Beledweyne** and click `Save`. Now click `Ok`([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_non_spatial_queries_wiki.html#save-selected-features-as-a-new-file)). In this exercise, we will not reproject the layers and work with the data in `ESPG:4326 - WGS84`.

trans-unit.source: Identify the building that might be affected by the flooding
trans-unit.target: Identify the building that might be affected by the flooding

trans-unit.source: In the following steps, we want to identify all buildings that are likely to be affected by the recent flooding. To do that we will use the tool `Extract by Location`. :::{figure} /fig/Extract_by_location_Belet_Weyne.png
trans-unit.target: In the following steps, we want to identify all buildings that are likely to be affected by the recent flooding. To do that we will use the tool `Extract by Location`. :::{figure} /fig/Extract_by_location_Belet_Weyne.png

trans-unit.source: The extract by extraction window in QGIS 3.36. :::
trans-unit.target: The extract by extraction window in QGIS 3.36. :::

trans-unit.source: In the __"Processing Toolbox"__ → Search for `Extract by Location`.
trans-unit.target: In the __"Processing Toolbox"__ → Search for `Extract by Location`.

trans-unit.source: __"Extract features from"__: `Buildings_Belete_Weyne.geojson`
trans-unit.target: __"Extract features from"__: `Buildings_Belete_Weyne.geojson`

trans-unit.source: __"Where the features (geometric predicate)"__: `are within`
trans-unit.target: __"Where the features (geometric predicate)"__: `are within`

trans-unit.source: __"By comparing to the features from"__: `GF2_20231123_FloodExtent_BeledweyneCity_HiraanRegion.shp`
trans-unit.target: __"By comparing to the features from"__: `GF2_20231123_FloodExtent_BeledweyneCity_HiraanRegion.shp`

trans-unit.source: Under `Extracted` click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png) → `Save to File...` and navigate to your **temp folder** and save the new layer under the name **Beledweyne_buildings_affected** and click `Save`.
trans-unit.target: Under `Extracted` click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png) → `Save to File...` and navigate to your **temp folder** and save the new layer under the name **Beledweyne_buildings_affected** and click `Save`.

trans-unit.source: Now, click `Run`.
trans-unit.target: Now, click `Run`.

trans-unit.source: Adjust your layers in a way that you only see the flooded areas and your new layer. **Beledweyne_buildings_affected**. Remove the `som_admbnda_adm2_ocha_20230308.shp` and `Buildings_Belete_Weyne.geojson` layer.
trans-unit.target: Adjust your layers in a way that you only see the flooded areas and your new layer. **Beledweyne_buildings_affected**. Remove the `som_admbnda_adm2_ocha_20230308.shp` and `Buildings_Belete_Weyne.geojson` layer.

trans-unit.source: Identify critical infrastructure affected by the floods
trans-unit.target: Identify critical infrastructure affected by the floods

trans-unit.source: In the next step, we want to identify special buildings among the affected buildings. Open the attribute table and check what kind of buildings can be found in the layer. This information can be found in the column "building". You can sort this column. To extract "hospitals", "schools", and "mosques", we can use the tool `Extract by Expression`.
trans-unit.target: In the next step, we want to identify special buildings among the affected buildings. Open the attribute table and check what kind of buildings can be found in the layer. This information can be found in the column "building". You can sort this column. To extract "hospitals", "schools", and "mosques", we can use the tool `Extract by Expression`.

trans-unit.source: Find the tool `Extract by Expression` in the `Toolbox`.
trans-unit.target: Find the tool `Extract by Expression` in the `Toolbox`.

trans-unit.source: `Expression`: click on ![](/fig/miconexpression.png).
trans-unit.target: `Expression`: click on ![](/fig/miconexpression.png).

trans-unit.source: The window "Expression" will open. Here we can build a very specific query. In the central panel open `Field and values`. Here you can see all the columns oft he the layer. Click on `building`. On the right-hand side, you should now see the option `All unique`. Click on it. Here you can see now all unique values in the column „building“.
trans-unit.target: The window "Expression" will open. Here we can build a very specific query. In the central panel open `Field and values`. Here you can see all the columns oft he the layer. Click on `building`. On the right-hand side, you should now see the option `All unique`. Click on it. Here you can see now all unique values in the column „building“.

trans-unit.source: In the `Expression` field, enter the following expression (see the figure below):
trans-unit.target: In the `Expression` field, enter the following expression (see the figure below):

trans-unit.source: Click `Ok`. The window will close and you will see the expression you created in the `Expression`-field in the `Extract by Expression` window (see figure below).
trans-unit.target: Click `Ok`. The window will close and you will see the expression you created in the `Expression`-field in the `Extract by Expression` window (see figure below).

trans-unit.source: Click `Run`. A new temporary layer called `Matching Features` will be added to your QGIS-project. Close the `Extract by Expression` window.
trans-unit.target: Click `Run`. A new temporary layer called `Matching Features` will be added to your QGIS-project. Close the `Extract by Expression` window.

trans-unit.source: The expression window in QGIS 3.36 with an expression to extract the polygons with the "buildings" value 'hospital', 'school', and 'mosque'. :::
trans-unit.target: The expression window in QGIS 3.36 with an expression to extract the polygons with the "buildings" value 'hospital', 'school', and 'mosque'. :::

trans-unit.source: The `Extract by Expression` window in QGIS 3.36 :::
trans-unit.target: The `Extract by Expression` window in QGIS 3.36 :::

trans-unit.source: Explore the new layer by opening the attribute table, activating and deactivating the layer in the Layers panel.
trans-unit.target: Explore the new layer by opening the attribute table, activating and deactivating the layer in the Layers panel.

trans-unit.source: <kbd>Right Click</kbd> on the `Matching Features` layer and save it to your project folder under `/data/output/` with the name `Belet_Weyne_POI_affected.gpkg`.
trans-unit.target: <kbd>Right Click</kbd> on the `Matching Features` layer and save it to your project folder under `/data/output/` with the name `Belet_Weyne_POI_affected.gpkg`.

trans-unit.source: Congratulations! The extracted information can now be used to perform further analyses or create comprehensive maps of the affected points of interest.
trans-unit.target: Congratulations! The extracted information can now be used to perform further analyses or create comprehensive maps of the affected points of interest.
